revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:c]] [[Category: c]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}}

The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|12=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|12=draft}}. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|12=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|12=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|12=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|12=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|12=draft}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|12=draft}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|12=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|12=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|12=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|12=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|12=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|12=draft|12=draft}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|12=draft|12=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft|12=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|12=draft|12=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}}

Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft|12=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|12=draft|12=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft|12=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|12=draft|12=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 佐紀陵山古墳 |画像= |所属=[[佐紀盾列古墳群]] |所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]山陵町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 58.76 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |形状= [[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長207m　 |築造年代= [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日葉酢媛命]] |出土品=銅鏡・埴輪 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「狭木之寺間陵」 |特記事項=全国第32位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 佐紀陵山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（左上）、'''佐紀陵山古墳'''（右上）、[[佐紀高塚古墳]]（左下）（1979年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[日葉酢媛命]]狭木之寺間陵 拝所}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|日葉酢媛命狭木之寺間陵 拝所の全天球画像}} '''佐紀陵山古墳'''（さきみささぎやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[山陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''狭木之寺間陵'''（さきのてらまのみささぎ、狹木之寺間陵）」として第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇后の[[日葉酢媛命]]の陵に治定されている。[[大正時代]]に盗掘事件が発生し、それに伴って当時としてはかなりの規模の調査が実施されたとされる。 == 概要 == [[殉死]]のかわりに初めて[[埴輪]]を御陵に立てたという説話が[[日本書紀]]に記されているのがこの古墳ではないかとされている。すぐに西側に接して築かれている佐紀石塚山古墳のくびれ部に佐紀陵山古墳の後円部がくい込んだ配置になっている。このため佐紀石塚山古墳の周濠は極端に狭くなっており、佐紀陵山古墳の方が先に築かれたことが推定されている。全長207メートル、前方部幅約87メートル、後円部径131メートル、前方部高さ12.3メートル、後円部高さ約20メートルの規模の三段に築成された古墳である河上「大和の古墳」(1984)p.42‐43。 [[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）に大がかりな盗掘をうけ、遺物が持ち出された。この事件はそののち犯人が検挙され、出土遺物は回収されている。翌年に[[宮内省]]により、復旧工事が行なわれ、それに伴い、陵墓という性格から大きな制約があったと推察されるものの、[[石室]]付近と出土[[遺物]]に関するかなり詳細な調査と記録の作成がなされたようである。 === 盗掘事件復旧工事に伴う調査 === 当時の復旧工事記録の大部分は宮内省本省にあったため、[[関東大震災]]で焼失したが、その記録のかなりの部分の写しを出土品の調査、整理にあたった[[京都大学]]考古学教室の[[梅原末治]]が手元に残しており、それが宮内庁書陵部に保管されている。また、出土遺物は復旧のさいに石室内に埋め戻されたが、写真や[[拓本]]、寒天型より作成された石膏模造品などにして残されている。復旧工事に伴う調査には梅原以外にも当時、東京帝室博物館（現在の東京国立博物館、当時は宮内省の管轄であった）に勤務していた和田千吉という考古学者も参加し、後円部頂上の埴輪を樹立した埋葬施設の復原図の試案なども作成していたという中井「日本の古代遺跡」（1982）pp.145-146森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)pｐ.117‐122。 === 石室構造 === 後円部頂上中央に存在する方形区画の真下につくられた[[竪穴式石室]]は、主軸をほぼ南北に持ち、長さ8.55メートル、幅1.09メートルという巨大なものである。その構築法は東西の長い側壁は扁平な割石を小口積みするもので普通の竪穴式石室とかわらないが、南北の短い側壁は大きな一枚石でつくられており、しかもこの石の上半中央部には孔が開けられていた。このような石室例は大阪府[[柏原市]]の[[松岳山古墳]]にその類例がみられるだけで、きわめて特異なものである。また、内部には長大な木棺を蔵していたと推測される（調査当時は腐朽し消滅）。この石室の天井石5枚あって、それぞれ前後の短側石に縄突起を付けていた。このような縄突起は古墳時代中期になると長持形石棺の蓋石や長側石に付けられることが多い。さらに、この天井石の上に屋根形をした石棺の蓋のように見える大型の石が置かれており、表面に直線の平行文様が線刻されている。魔除けの一種として施されたものではないかと考えられる森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p.118。 === 石室付近の埴輪群 === [[ファイル:佐紀陵山古墳出土 蓋形埴輪 (複製).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|蓋形埴輪（複製）}}{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}}]] 石室上部は2メートルほどに土を盛って小さな円形の盛土を造り、その上に7 - 8個のキヌガサ形埴輪と数個の盾形埴輪を立てていた。とくに土檀中央の最高所に立てられたキヌガサ形埴輪は大きく、稀に見る丁寧さで作られていた（高さ1.5メートル、横幅2メートル）。キヌガサの上部には、複雑な直弧文が一周しさらに四個の突出した部分（ヒレ）をも直弧文で飾っていた。盾形埴輪も丁寧な作りで木製の盾を模したものと推測され、周縁全部と中央の二本の横帯をやはり直弧文で飾っていた。なお、盾形埴輪は高さ108センチメートル、最大幅80センチメートルである。これらの一部は石膏模型にされて東京国立博物館（当時は帝室博物館）に残されている森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 118 ‐124。 === 出土遺物と年代 === 石室内からの出土遺物としては # 銅鏡は流雲文縁変形[[方格規矩鏡]][[仿製鏡]]、唐草文縁変形方格規矩鏡、直弧文縁変形[[内行花文鏡]]があるが、これらの三面は32センチメートル - 34センチメートルの直径があり、舶載鏡（中国鏡）よりかなり大きく製作された仿製鏡で、石膏模型、写真、拓本が残っている。また写真ではこの他に平縁式四獣鏡があり、これ以外にも1もしくは2面の鏡があったようであるが、他の古墳から盗掘したものを犯人が所持していた可能性もあるという。 # 石製腕飾り類は車輪石３、鍬形石3、石釧1があった。 # 石製模造品には刀子3、斧1、高坏2、椅子1がある。 # 他には管玉1、琴柱形石製品、石製臼1などが出土している森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 122中井「日本の古代遺跡」（1982）p.146。 以上、埴輪や石室内の遺物の構成から見て古墳時代前期でも終わりに近い年代が考えられるという森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 124。 === 各地の佐紀陵山型古墳 === {{Location map+|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|width=220|float=right|caption={{center|佐紀陵山型古墳の分布}}{{small|1：'''佐紀陵山古墳'''
2：[[五色塚古墳]]
3：[[摩湯山古墳]]
4：[[茶臼山古墳 (大津市)|膳所茶臼山古墳]]
5：[[御墓山古墳]]}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=58.76|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=20.63|mark=前方後円墳-南.svg|marksize=20|label={{color|#FF0000|'''1'''}}|label_size=100|position=bottom}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=46.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=45.08|mark=前方後円墳-南南西.svg|marksize=20|label=2|label_size=100|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=27|lat_sec=44.70|lon_deg=135|lon_min=26|lon_sec=0.05|mark=前方後円墳-北西.svg|marksize=20|label=3|label_size=100|position=bottom}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=25.17|lon_deg=135|lon_min=53|lon_sec=2.45|mark=前方後円墳-東.svg|marksize=20|label=4|label_size=100|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=48|lat_sec=6.10|lon_deg=136|lon_min=10|lon_sec=23.77|mark=前方後円墳-北北東.svg|marksize=20|label=5|label_size=100|position=right}} }} 佐紀陵山古墳と相似形の古墳は、[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]]）・[[茶臼山古墳 (大津市)|膳所茶臼山古墳]]（[[滋賀県]]）・[[御墓山古墳]]（[[三重県]]）・[[摩湯山古墳]]（[[大阪府]]）など各地で知られる。これら「佐紀陵山型」前方後円墳が畿内を取り囲むように分布することから、この4世紀後半当時に畿内制的な領域支配が存在したとする説がある下垣仁志による説（菱田哲郎『古代日本国家形成の考古学』京都大学学術出版会、2007年、p. 74より）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[河上邦彦]]「日葉酢媛命陵」『大和の古墳を語る』　六興出版　1984年　42頁‐43頁 * 中井一夫「佐紀陵山古墳」『日本の古代遺跡4　奈良北部』　保育社　1982年　144頁‐146頁 * [[森浩一]]「佐紀陵山古墳の盗掘事件と後円部の様子」『天皇陵古墳への招待』　筑摩書房2011年117頁‐124頁 == 関連項目 == *[[五色塚古墳]] - 佐紀陵山古墳と同規格とされる。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html 佐紀陵山古墳 大和の遺跡]（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]） {{デフォルトソート:さきみささきやまこふん}} [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（左上）、'''佐紀陵山古墳'''（右上）、[[佐紀高塚古墳]]（左下）（1979年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[日葉酢媛命]]狭木之寺間陵 拝所}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|日葉酢媛命狭木之寺間陵 拝所の全天球画像}} '''佐紀陵山古墳'''（さきみささぎやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[山陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''狭木之寺間陵'''（さきのてらまのみささぎ、狹木之寺間陵）」として第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇后の[[日葉酢媛命]]の陵に治定されている。[[大正時代]]に盗掘事件が発生し、それに伴って当時としてはかなりの規模の調査が実施されたとされる。 == 概要 == [[殉死]]のかわりに初めて[[埴輪]]を御陵に立てたという説話が[[日本書紀]]に記されているのがこの古墳ではないかとされている。すぐに西側に接して築かれている佐紀石塚山古墳のくびれ部に佐紀陵山古墳の後円部がくい込んだ配置になっている。このため佐紀石塚山古墳の周濠は極端に狭くなっており、佐紀陵山古墳の方が先に築かれたことが推定されている。全長207メートル、前方部幅約87メートル、後円部径131メートル、前方部高さ12.3メートル、後円部高さ約20メートルの規模の三段に築成された古墳である河上「大和の古墳」(1984)p.42‐43。 [[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）に大がかりな盗掘をうけ、遺物が持ち出された。この事件はそののち犯人が検挙され、出土遺物は回収されている。翌年に[[宮内省]]により、復旧工事が行なわれ、それに伴い、陵墓という性格から大きな制約があったと推察されるものの、[[石室]]付近と出土[[遺物]]に関するかなり詳細な調査と記録の作成がなされたようである。 === 盗掘事件復旧工事に伴う調査 === 当時の復旧工事記録の大部分は宮内省本省にあったため、[[関東大震災]]で焼失したが、その記録のかなりの部分の写しを出土品の調査、整理にあたった[[京都大学]]考古学教室の[[梅原末治]]が手元に残しており、それが宮内庁書陵部に保管されている。また、出土遺物は復旧のさいに石室内に埋め戻されたが、写真や[[拓本]]、寒天型より作成された石膏模造品などにして残されている。復旧工事に伴う調査には梅原以外にも当時、東京帝室博物館（現在の東京国立博物館、当時は宮内省の管轄であった）に勤務していた和田千吉という考古学者も参加し、後円部頂上の埴輪を樹立した埋葬施設の復原図の試案なども作成していたという中井「日本の古代遺跡」（1982）pp.145-146森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)pｐ.117‐122。 === 石室構造 === 後円部頂上中央に存在する方形区画の真下につくられた[[竪穴式石室]]は、主軸をほぼ南北に持ち、長さ8.55メートル、幅1.09メートルという巨大なものである。その構築法は東西の長い側壁は扁平な割石を小口積みするもので普通の竪穴式石室とかわらないが、南北の短い側壁は大きな一枚石でつくられており、しかもこの石の上半中央部には孔が開けられていた。このような石室例は大阪府[[柏原市]]の[[松岳山古墳]]にその類例がみられるだけで、きわめて特異なものである。また、内部には長大な木棺を蔵していたと推測される（調査当時は腐朽し消滅）。この石室の天井石5枚あって、それぞれ前後の短側石に縄突起を付けていた。このような縄突起は古墳時代中期になると長持形石棺の蓋石や長側石に付けられることが多い。さらに、この天井石の上に屋根形をした石棺の蓋のように見える大型の石が置かれており、表面に直線の平行文様が線刻されている。魔除けの一種として施されたものではないかと考えられる森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p.118。 === 石室付近の埴輪群 === [[ファイル:佐紀陵山古墳出土 蓋形埴輪 (複製).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|蓋形埴輪（複製）}}{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}}]] 石室上部は2メートルほどに土を盛って小さな円形の盛土を造り、その上に7 - 8個のキヌガサ形埴輪と数個の盾形埴輪を立てていた。とくに土檀中央の最高所に立てられたキヌガサ形埴輪は大きく、稀に見る丁寧さで作られていた（高さ1.5メートル、横幅2メートル）。キヌガサの上部には、複雑な直弧文が一周しさらに四個の突出した部分（ヒレ）をも直弧文で飾っていた。盾形埴輪も丁寧な作りで木製の盾を模したものと推測され、周縁全部と中央の二本の横帯をやはり直弧文で飾っていた。なお、盾形埴輪は高さ108センチメートル、最大幅80センチメートルである。これらの一部は石膏模型にされて東京国立博物館（当時は帝室博物館）に残されている森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 118 ‐124。 === 出土遺物と年代 === 石室内からの出土遺物としては # 銅鏡は流雲文縁変形[[方格規矩鏡]][[仿製鏡]]、唐草文縁変形方格規矩鏡、直弧文縁変形[[内行花文鏡]]があるが、これらの三面は32センチメートル - 34センチメートルの直径があり、舶載鏡（中国鏡）よりかなり大きく製作された仿製鏡で、石膏模型、写真、拓本が残っている。また写真ではこの他に平縁式四獣鏡があり、これ以外にも1もしくは2面の鏡があったようであるが、他の古墳から盗掘したものを犯人が所持していた可能性もあるという。 # 石製腕飾り類は車輪石３、鍬形石3、石釧1があった。 # 石製模造品には刀子3、斧1、高坏2、椅子1がある。 # 他には管玉1、琴柱形石製品、石製臼1などが出土している森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 122中井「日本の古代遺跡」（1982）p.146。 以上、埴輪や石室内の遺物の構成から見て古墳時代前期でも終わりに近い年代が考えられるという森「天皇陵古墳」(2011)p. 124。 === 各地の佐紀陵山型古墳 === {{Location map+|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|width=220|float=right|caption={{center|佐紀陵山型古墳の分布}}{{small|1：'''佐紀陵山古墳'''
2：[[五色塚古墳]]
3：[[摩湯山古墳]]
4：[[茶臼山古墳 (大津市)|膳所茶臼山古墳]]
5：[[御墓山古墳]]}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=58.76|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=20.63|mark=前方後円墳-南.svg|marksize=20|label={{color|#FF0000|'''1'''}}|label_size=100|position=bottom}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=46.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=45.08|mark=前方後円墳-南南西.svg|marksize=20|label=2|label_size=100|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=27|lat_sec=44.70|lon_deg=135|lon_min=26|lon_sec=0.05|mark=前方後円墳-北西.svg|marksize=20|label=3|label_size=100|position=bottom}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=25.17|lon_deg=135|lon_min=53|lon_sec=2.45|mark=前方後円墳-東.svg|marksize=20|label=4|label_size=100|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan zoom Tokai, Hokuriku, and Kansai|lat_deg=34|lat_min=48|lat_sec=6.10|lon_deg=136|lon_min=10|lon_sec=23.77|mark=前方後円墳-北北東.svg|marksize=20|label=5|label_size=100|position=right}} }} 佐紀陵山古墳と相似形の古墳は、[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]]）・[[茶臼山古墳 (大津市)|膳所茶臼山古墳]]（[[滋賀県]]）・[[御墓山古墳]]（[[三重県]]）・[[摩湯山古墳]]（[[大阪府]]）など各地で知られる。これら「佐紀陵山型」前方後円墳が畿内を取り囲むように分布することから、この4世紀後半当時に畿内制的な領域支配が存在したとする説がある下垣仁志による説（菱田哲郎『古代日本国家形成の考古学』京都大学学術出版会、2007年、p. 74より）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[河上邦彦]]「日葉酢媛命陵」『大和の古墳を語る』　六興出版　1984年　42頁‐43頁 * 中井一夫「佐紀陵山古墳」『日本の古代遺跡4　奈良北部』　保育社　1982年　144頁‐146頁 * [[森浩一]]「佐紀陵山古墳の盗掘事件と後円部の様子」『天皇陵古墳への招待』　筑摩書房2011年117頁‐124頁 == 関連項目 == *[[五色塚古墳]] - 佐紀陵山古墳と同規格とされる。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html 佐紀陵山古墳 大和の遺跡]（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]） {{デフォルトソート:さきみささきやまこふん}} [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hyogo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fangzhuo|qq=}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshikizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fangzhuo|qq=}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshikizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

|image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}} Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fangzhuo|qq=}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshikizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]
