Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fangzhuo|qq=}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshikizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

|image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} records the first instance of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three {{ill|fangzhuo|ja|方格規矩鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fangzhuo|qq=}}, including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|ja|五色塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshikizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Kofun" to "Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} |Location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]]

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} |Location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} |Location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group�]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara�]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan |Name = {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryōzan Kofun|qq=}} |image = |Part of = {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} |Location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] Yamamori-cho |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 41 |Latitude_sec = 58.76 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 47 |Longitude_sec = 20.63 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |Size = Total mound length: 207 m |Construction = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Burial = {{ill|Vertical shaft chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical shaft chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Deity = (Determined by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]

{{ill|Hibasuhime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, haniwa |Imperial_tomb = {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Notes = Ranked 32nd in Japan for size[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Kofun Size Rankings (Japan)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Nara City |Label = Saki Ryoyama
Kofun |Label_position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-south |location = {{ill|Misasagi-chō|qid=Q85879846}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.69965555555556|N|135.78906388888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} |commemorates = {{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|qid=Q8375821}} |part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489}} }} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (top left), '''Saki Ryoyama Kofun''' (top right), {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (bottom left) (1979)
{{National Air Photograph}}]] [[File:Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}} Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum Shrine.}}]] {{panoViewer|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|360-degree image of Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}} '''Saki Misasagiyama Kofun''' (佐紀陵山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Yamamori-cho. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the {{ill|Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|ja|狭木之寺間陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki-no-Terama Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the resting place of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, empress of the 11th emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. A significant looting event in the Taisho era prompted extensive investigations and restoration. == Overview == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records the first instance of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] being placed on this kofun instead of human sacrifice. Saki Ryoyama Kofun's rear mound encroaches into the constricted moat of the adjacent {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting the latter was constructed earlier. The mound measures 207 meters in total length, with a rear circle diameter of 131 meters and height of 20 meters, while the front section spans 87 meters in width and rises 12.3 meters.Kawakami, "Kofun of Yamato" (1984), pp. 42–43. In 1915, a large-scale looting incident resulted in the theft of artifacts. The perpetrators were later arrested, and the stolen artifacts were recovered. The following year, restoration works by the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Agency]] included detailed surveys and records of the burial chamber and recovered items despite restrictions due to the tomb's status.Nakai, "Ancient Ruins of Japan" (1982), pp. 145–146. === Investigation During Restoration === Most records from the restoration, originally held by the Imperial Household Agency in Tokyo, were lost in the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. However, copies and artifact replicas made under archaeologists such as {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}} at Kyoto University were preserved and are now housed at the {{ill|Imperial Household Archives|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Archives|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}}. Artifact casts, photos, and sketches remain, while looted items were returned to the tomb. === Chamber Structure === The burial chamber lies beneath the central mound and measures 8.55 meters in length and 1.09 meters in width. Constructed with flat stones and capped by five roof stones, it is one of the largest vertical shaft chambers in Japan. The southern and northern walls each feature a single massive stone slab with unique holes.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), pp. 117–124. === Artifacts and Dating === Artifacts recovered include: * Bronze mirrors: Three [[:en:TLV mirror|fangzhuo]], including cloud-patterned and floral designs. * Stone ornaments: Rings, tools, and vessels. * Rare haniwa, including shield-shaped and cap-shaped examples.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 122. These suggest the kofun dates to the late early Kofun period.Mori, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (2011), p. 124. == Related Sites == Similar kofun include: * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Chausuyama Kofun|ja|茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Otsu City, Shiga Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kawakami Kunihiko|lt=Kunihiko Kawakami|ja|河上邦彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunihiko Kawakami|qq=}}, "The Tomb of Hibasuhime," in ''Discussing Yamato's Kofun'' (Rokko Publishing, 1984), pp. 42–43. * {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, "Ancient Imperial Tombs" (Chikuma Publishing, 2011), pp. 117–124. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|364093588}} * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html] {{translated page|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|version=98265439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Saki Misasagiyama Kofun]] [[sn:Saki Ryōzan Kofun]] [[ja:佐紀陵山古墳]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun}} [[zh:佐紀陵山古墳]]
